name: Apple Foundation Model 3 Core (on-device, ~3B)
archDisplay: Dense on-device transformer, third generation of Apple's on-device model line
note: Third-generation on-device model announced at WWDC26 (2026-06-08), a quality step up from the 2025 on-device model; gains image-input understanding via the Foundation Models framework's new multimodal update.
verificationNotes:
Confirmed via Apple's own machinelearning.apple.com research post, 9to5Mac's WWDC26 explainer, and MacRumors' June 9, 2026 coverage of the Platforms State of the Union. ctxK=32 is CARRIED FORWARD from the prior-generation on-device model (Apple did not publish a new context-window figure for this generation in the sources found) — flagged as an estimate, not a confirmed new number. No standard-benchmark (MMLU/GPQA-style) numeric scores were published for this generation — Apple reported only internal human-preference win-rates (e.g. 45.6% vs 23.3% preferred over the 2025 model on text tasks), which are not on the fixed benchmark-slug list, so no 'results' rows are included rather than mis-mapping them. This is a genuine gap: the corpus's Apple folder only has the original ~3B on-device model from June 2024. ROUND-7 EXHAUSTIVE-SEARCH RECORD (2026-07-15, methodology-diversified per native-language/regional-leaderboard instructions): No new benchmark results found for Apple Foundation Model 3 Core (on-device, ~3B) — corpus file corpus/models/apple/apple-foundation-model-3-core-on-device-3b.json remains at results: [].

Search methodology (widened per instructions): Apple is a US vendor, so Chinese/Japanese-source search angles (point 1/2 of the brief) don't apply. Focused on point 3 instead:
- Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai): no mention of any Apple Foundation Model in leaderboards or model pages — Apple's on-device models aren't API-accessible outside Apple's own devices/frameworks, so AA has no way to benchmark them.
- LMArena/LMSYS: no Apple Foundation Model entries; the July-2026 leaderboard is dominated by Claude/GPT/Gemini frontier models, and edge/on-device coverage there is Qwen/Gemma, not Apple.
- OpenRouter/HuggingFace: no AFM-3 model card or listing (the model isn't distributed as open weights or via API).
- Direct fetch of Apple's own launch post (machinelearning.apple.com/research/introducing-third-generation-of-apple-foundation-models): confirmed again it reports ONLY human-preference win-rates (e.g., 45.6% vs 23.3% preferred over 2025 baseline; TTS MOS scores; 61% image-understanding preference) — explicitly no MMLU/GPQA/IFEval/SWE-bench numbers for the 3rd-gen models.
- Checked machinelearning.apple.com/updates/ for any newer Apple research post (June–July 2026) that might contain the promised follow-up tech report — none found yet; multiple secondary sources (ofox.ai developer read, 9to5Mac) note Apple said it would publish "updated evaluations and benchmarks" in a technical report "later this summer" (2026), which as of today (2026-07-15) does not appear to have been released yet.
- Found and ruled out a false lead: an AppleLeaker tweet and several SEO-farm articles cite "AFM on-device: MMLU 67.8, IFEval 85.1, MMMLU 60.60, MGSM 74.91, beats Qwen-2.5-3B/Gemma-3-4B/Gemma-3n-E4B." Traced this to arXiv 2507.13575 ("Apple Intelligence Foundation Language Models: Tech Report 2025", published July 2025) and confirmed via Twitter snowflake-ID decoding that the tweet itself dates to ~July 2025 — these numbers describe the PREVIOUS (2024/2025) on-device AFM generation, not the June-2026 third-generation AFM 3 Core that is the subject of this file. Did not apply them to avoid mis-attribution.
- Wayback Machine: not separately queried because Apple's launch page is live and current (not a dead page needing archival recovery); the live page was already fetched directly and is the authoritative source.

Conclusion: this remains a genuine, expected gap — Apple has not yet published any standard fixed-benchmark-slug numeric results for the 3rd-generation on-device model as of 2026-07-15. The promised follow-up technical report (which historically is where Apple publishes MMLU/MGSM/IFEval-style numbers, per the 2025 precedent at arXiv 2507.13575) has apparently not shipped yet. Recommend re-checking machinelearning.apple.com/research/ periodically over the coming weeks/months, since that report — once published — is very likely to contain benchmark-slug-mappable results (MMLU, MGSM, IFEval, MMMLU are the exact metrics Apple used last cycle). ROUND-13 STRUCTURED-SOURCE CHECK (2026-07-15, Epoch AI raw benchmark feed + Vals.ai leaderboards, checked directly): Checked the two specified structured sources for Apple Foundation Model 3 Core (on-device, ~3B) — corpus file corpus/models/apple/apple-foundation-model-3-core-on-device-3b.json — and found no results in either.

1. Epoch AI raw benchmark data feed:
   - Fetched https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.json (179KB) directly. This file is benchmark-definition metadata only (title, category, description, methodology per benchmark slug like gpqa-diamond, frontiermath-tier-4-v1, etc.) — it contains no per-model scores, so searched it anyway for "apple"/"afm"/"foundation model" strings: zero matches.
   - Fetched https://epoch.ai/data/benchmarks.csv (2.3MB, 5981 rows) — this is the actual per-model-run results feed (columns: model, Organization, Hugging Face developer id, benchmark, score, etc.).
